title: quercus ithaburensis
intro: Quercus ithaburensis, also known as the Lebanese oak, is a species of deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of Lebanon and Syria. The bark and leaves of the quercus ithaburensis plant are used in traditional medicine, known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of this plant have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and improving cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of quercus ithaburensis are typically made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves that can be used to treat various health conditions.
